title: Understand: genes, proteins, and traits
description: Check your understanding of genes, proteins, and traits in this set of free, standards-aligned practice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about the relationship between genes and proteins is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The order of nucleotides in a gene determines the order of amino acids in a protein.
The order of amino acids in a gene determines the order of nucleotides in a protein.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about the relationship between genes and proteins is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The order of nucleotides in a gene determines the order of amino acids in a protein.
The order of nucleotides in a protein determines the order of amino acids in a gene.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about the relationship between genes and proteins is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The order of amino acids in a protein is determined by the order of nucleotides in a gene.
The order of nucleotides in a protein is determined by the order of amino acids in a gene.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the relationship between genes, proteins, and traits?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Genes provide instructions for making proteins, which affect an organism’s traits.
Proteins provide instructions for making genes, which affect an organism’s traits.
Genes affect an organism’s traits, but proteins do not.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the relationship between genes, proteins, and traits?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Genes encode proteins, which help determine an organism’s traits.
Proteins encode genes, which help determine an organism’s traits.
Proteins affect an organism’s traits, but genes do not.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the relationship between genes, proteins, and traits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Genes tell the cell how to make proteins, and proteins affect an organism’s traits.
Proteins tell the cell how to make genes, and genes affect an organism’s traits.
Genes affect an organism’s traits, but proteins do not.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about proteins is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Proteins carry out many different functions in the cell.
The order of amino acids in a protein affects its shape, but not its function.
Proteins function inside cells, and so do not affect an organism’s visible traits.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about proteins is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Proteins carry out a single function in the cell.
A protein’s amino acids affect its shape and its function.
Proteins function inside cells, and so do not affect an organism’s visible traits.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about proteins is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Proteins carry out a single function in the cell.
The order of amino acids in a protein affects its shape, but not its function.
The ways proteins function in the cell help determine an organism’s traits.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about genes is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Genes are part of larger structures called chromosomes.
Genes are made up of DNA nucleotides called A, C, L, and S.
Having different genes usually causes organisms to have the same traits.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about genes is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Genes are part of smaller structures called chromosomes.
Genes are made up of DNA nucleotides called A, C, T, and G.
Having different genes usually causes organisms to have the same traits.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about genes is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Genes are part of smaller structures called chromosomes.
Genes are made up of DNA nucleotides called L, P, T, and G.
Having different genes can cause organisms to have different traits.
